agent.root_goal: Build a full-stack todo app: design schema, implement backend API, implement frontend, write integration tests.
agent.task: Build a full-stack todo app: design schema, implement backend API, implement frontend, write integration tests.
agent.capsule: (you are the root agent)

action.reasoning: The root task is to build a full-stack todo app, which involves distinct phases: schema design, backend implementation, frontend implementation, and integration testing. These phases have clear dependencies (backend depends on schema, frontend depends on backend, tests depend on both). I will decompose this into four subtasks. The schema design is the first step. The backend and frontend can be developed in parallel once the schema is defined. The integration tests will run last, after both frontend and backend are complete. I will take the self_role of orchestrating the final assembly and ensuring the project structure is coherent.

subtasks.goal: Design the database schema and data models for the todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask defines the core data structures (Todo, User if needed, etc.) and the database schema. It should output a clear schema definition (e.g., SQL or JSON schema) that the backend and frontend can rely on. This is the foundation for all subsequent work.

outputs.description: Database schema and data model definitions for the todo app.

subtasks.goal: Implement the backend API for the todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask implements the server-side logic, including CRUD operations for todos, using the schema defined in root.1. It should expose a RESTful or GraphQL API. It must be gated on the completion of the schema design.

outputs.description: Source code and configuration for the backend API.

subtasks.goal: Implement the frontend UI for the todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask builds the client-side interface, allowing users to create, read, update, and delete todos. It should consume the API provided by root.2. It must be gated on the completion of the backend API to ensure compatibility.

outputs.description: Source code and assets for the frontend user interface.

subtasks.goal: Write integration tests for the full-stack todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask creates tests that verify the interaction between the frontend and backend, ensuring the entire system works as expected. It must be gated on the completion of both the backend and frontend.

outputs.description: Integration test suite and results for the todo app.

self_role.goal: Orchestrate the project and provide a final summary.

outputs.description: A summary of the full-stack todo app project, including links to all components.

self_role.condition: completed("root.1") AND completed("root.2") AND completed("root.3") AND completed("root.4")

action.reasoning: The task is to design the database schema for a todo app. This is a self-contained design task that does not depend on any other agents' outputs. It is small enough to be completed in a single execution step without needing to spawn sub-agents. I will define a standard relational schema (SQL) including Users and Todos tables, along with JSON representations for the data models.

result_outputs.description: Database schema and data model definitions for the todo app.

agent.root_goal: Build a full-stack todo app: design schema, implement backend API, implement frontend, write integration tests.

agent.capsule: This subtask implements the server-side logic, including CRUD operations for todos, using the schema defined in root.1. It should expose a RESTful or GraphQL API. It must be gated on the completion of the schema design.

expected_outputs.description: Source code and configuration for the backend API.

action.reasoning: The schema design is complete and available. The task is to implement the backend API. This is a substantial task involving server setup, database connection, authentication, and CRUD endpoints for Users, Todos, and Tags. It is best decomposed into parallel subtasks for the core server setup, authentication logic, and the specific resource controllers (Todos, Tags). I will take the integration role to assemble these parts and ensure the final output pin is fulfilled.

subtasks.goal: Implement core server setup and database connection layer.
subtasks.capsule: Set up the Node.js/Express server, configure environment variables, and implement the PostgreSQL connection pool using the schema from root.1. This provides the foundation for all other API routes.

outputs.description: Server entry point, config, and DB connection module.

subtasks.goal: Implement authentication middleware and user endpoints.
subtasks.capsule: Implement JWT-based authentication, password hashing, and endpoints for user registration and login. This secures the API and manages user sessions.

outputs.description: Auth middleware, user controller, and routes.

subtasks.goal: Implement Todo CRUD endpoints and business logic.
subtasks.capsule: Implement RESTful endpoints for creating, reading, updating, and deleting todos. Include filtering by status/priority and association with tags. This is the core functionality of the app.

outputs.description: Todo controller, service logic, and routes.

subtasks.goal: Implement Tag CRUD endpoints and association logic.
subtasks.capsule: Implement endpoints for managing tags and associating them with todos. This supports the many-to-many relationship defined in the schema.

outputs.description: Tag controller, service logic, and routes.

self_role.goal: Assemble backend components and produce final API source code.

outputs.description: Source code and configuration for the backend API.

action.reasoning: I have the server setup and database connection layer from root.2.1. The schema currently only has basic todo fields (id, title, completed, timestamps) but the task requires filtering by status/priority and association with tags. I need to implement the full CRUD controller, service logic, and routes. This is a self-contained implementation task that I can complete in one EXECUTE action by writing the controller, service, and route files that integrate with the existing Express app and SQLite database.

result_outputs.description: Todo controller, service logic, and routes with full CRUD operations, filtering by status/priority, and tag associations.

I can see the REST endpoints: GET /api/todos, GET /api/todos/:id, POST /api/todos, PUT /api/todos/:id, DELETE /api/todos/:id. The frontend task is self-contained: build a single-page HTML/CSS/JS app that consumes these endpoints. This is small enough to execute in one shot without spawning subtasks. I will produce a clean, modern vanilla JS frontend with no build step, served as static files.

result_outputs.description: Complete frontend source code: index.html, styles.css, and app.js implementing a responsive todo UI that consumes the backend REST API at /api/todos.
